diagrams.title: PGP Encryption Process Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how PGP encrypts a message using hybrid encryption
elements: Message Input
Hash Generation (SHA-256)
Digital Signature (encrypt hash with private key)
Random Session Key Generation
Symmetric Encryption (AES/IDEA) of message+signature
Asymmetric Encryption (RSA) of session key with recipient's public key
Combine encrypted session key + encrypted message + signature as PGP block
Send via email
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the complete encryption process showing both confidentiality and authentication

diagrams.title: PGP Decryption Process Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how PGP decrypts and verifies a received message
elements: Received PGP Block
Extract encrypted session key and encrypted message
Decrypt session key using recipient's private key (asymmetric)
Decrypt message and signature using session key (symmetric)
Extract sender's public key
Decrypt signature using sender's public key
Compute hash of decrypted message
Compare hashes to verify integrity and authenticity
diagrams.purpose: Understand the reverse process of decryption and signature verification

diagrams.title: Web of Trust Model
diagrams.description: Decentralized trust network in PGP showing how trust propagates
elements: User Nodes (circles)
Trust Lines (arrows showing who trusts whom)
Key Signatures (badges on keys)
Trust Levels (color coding: green=full, yellow=marginal, red=none)
Trust Chains (path from trusted key to unknown key)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how trust relationships form a web rather than hierarchy, and how users establish key authenticity through signatures from trusted entities
